rows.title: LASD CCW Modification Timeline Data Point
rows.body:
One of our students completed a LASD firearm modification qualification with us at Burro Canyon on Sunday, May 3.

He submitted his modification paperwork and training certificate the same day.

Timeline:
• May 3: Qualification completed and documents submitted
• May 11: Approved for pickup
• May 15: Pickup appointment completed

Student said the pickup process at LASD was extremely smooth. They used the existing CCW photo, printed the updated permit ahead of time, and the entire visit took about 5 minutes.

This is another indication LASD modifications are moving much faster than many people expect.

Reminder:
• LASD requires qualification/training documentation within 30 days
• You must list all firearms currently on the permit plus the new firearm being added
• Bring matching firearm information exactly as registered/DROS'd

For those asking, LASD modifications are definitely active right now.

rows.title: San Joaquin County CCW Timeline
rows.body:
This will just be a straight to the point start to finish on how long it took me to get my CCW in San Joaquin County. Mostly everything was done online.

December 5, 2025 : Complete application through San Joaquin County Sheriff and pay $163 including taxes. After the application through Permitium it will tell you to book an appointment for interview and Live Scan

December 9, 2025: I came in, an officer guided me through a metal detector. Waited at the lobby and then got called in. A person will sit you down and walkthrough your application making sure every information is correct. He then told me how long I should expect to get a call back, he said about 3-5 months. After the interview it is the Live Scan.

March 6, 2026 : I received a missed call from the county sheriff, he did leave a voicemail regarding the CCW application. I tried calling back he didn't answer so I left a voicemail when I was available. Also keep in mind this is when they have already contacted the 3 references that you are required to put on the application. My friends had let me know that they were called by the same guy.

March 10, 2026 : He calls again, I was expecting an interview. But it is almost exactly like the interview on December 9
th. He questions my job and about my application again. In the end he says I will get a call back in 3-4 weeks If I am approved or not.

March 21, 2026 : I got the call, this time it was automated and saying that I am approved and now allowed to take the 16 hour class.

March 21,22, 2026 : I took my 16 hour class with Elite Armory Plus in Tracy, completed it in a day and got my 16 hour class certificate. I call their store to book an appointment for the Live Fire.

March 27 2026 : Arrived at the store with my 3 firearms that I needed to qualify for my CCW. Basically 30 rounds for each firearm. 5 Rounds at 1.5 yards, 5 Rounds at 3 yards, 10 Rounds at 5 yards, 5 Rounds at 7 yards, 5 Rounds at 10 yards.

After passing the Live Fire they will give a paper certificate for qualification. So next I went on Permitium to upload my 16 hour class certificate and the Live Fire certificate. After all that I was able to schedule my pick up.

March 30, 2026 : I go to the Sheriffs Department, go through the metal detectors, wait in the lobby. I gave my ID and after 15 minutes I was out with my new CCW.So total around 3 months to get a CCW in San Joaquin county maybe shorter If I didn't miss his call hahahaha

rows.title: LASD CCW Modify (Add/Remove Firearms) – Early Turnaround Data Point
rows.body:
CCW

Now that LASD has finally rolled out the modify option, starting to see some real timelines come through.

Recent example:
• Qualification: April 18
• Submitted via Permitium same day
• Email to schedule pickup: April 24

~6 day turnaround

A few things that seem pretty clear so far:
• LASD is expecting a current qualification (they're not playing around with older certs)
• Process order matters: qualify first, then submit
• You need to list all firearms you want on your permit, not just the new/additional one
• Most delays I've heard about are from incomplete or outdated paperwork

Honestly faster than expected, but this is still early. Wouldn't be surprised if timelines change once volume picks up.

Curious what others are seeing so far. Anyone else submit a modify request yet? What was your turnaround?

rows.title: LAPD CCW Timeline (Oct. 2025 to April 2026)
rows.body:
CCW

Yet another CCW timeline through LAPD, hopefully gives a rough idea of what to expect as of now.

Total wait was 193 days and cost was $757.60.

—-

Late September 2025 - Turned 21

10/2/2025 - Submitted waitlist request
1/15/2026 - Received Permitium link to apply. Scheduled interview (for 4/27) and gave references. Paid $60.21

2/03/2026 - On my occasional Permitium check, I saw that an earlier interview was available. Rescheduled interview for 2/20.
2/20/2026 - Interview was super easy. Guy just let me know that he'd be calling my references soon. He also said that I should have done my live scan before the interview (which was news to me) but he was cool about it. Probably isn't normal, but he didn't ask any questions. Just had me sign my application and said I was good to go.
2/20/2026 - Went and got my Livescan done after my interview. Cost $110. Booked training (NationwideCCW in North Hollywood) for $349.

2/21-22/2026 - Class was simple. Classroom stuff for the first day, qualifying at the range for the second day. Super cool teacher, would recommend if you're in the area.
2/23/2026 - My references all got calls. I uploaded my training certificates to Permitium.
2/23/2026- Pending Supervisor Review
2/28/2026 - Pending Captain Review
3/12/2026 - Pending Chief Review
3/17/2026 - Pending Final Review
3/26/2026 - Approved
4/2/2026 - Made final payment (I moved between approval and now, so there was a bit of a delay waiting for them to confirm my new address). I was told that my permit has been issued and will be mailed to my new address. Paid $222.39

4/7/2026 - They backpedal and say that my permit hasn't been issued, and now they actually need me to go through the initial residence verification process again. Sent lease and (temp) drivers license with updated address.
4/13/2026 - Received permit with issuance date of 4/15.

Total cost: $757.60

Total wait: 193 days

rows.title: LAPD CCW timeline - 176 days
rows.body:
CCW

After reading horror stories about long waits (like 1-2 years) this felt relatively quick for LAPD. Just about 6 months from first email to card in hand.

October 9 - Email sent

January 22 - Recieved email to do the application. Filled it out and paid $53.

Did live scan same day, cost $119

February 10 - In person appointment

February 11, 12 - CCW class with Nationwide CCW. Cost $349. Uploaded certificate immediately.

February 18 - References called.

March 19 - Approved. Paid $222.39

April 3 - Received card in the mail. They send them out on the 1st and 15th of every month.

Total cost $743.39

Total time 176 days

Two things I didn't know about:

While they only need to call 3 references, you have to enter 5 so they have a couple of backups if they don't get ahold of someone. Wasn't a problem but it was a surprise when filling out the application so I quickly had to ask a couple of more people if I could use them as references.

The firearms you qualify with don't have to be the same you entered on the application. What you qualify with is what matters.

I changed one of my 3 guns at qualification because I changed my mind and it wasn't a problem.

rows.title: CCW Timeline - LAPD
rows.body:
21 days

Here's the breakdown of my timeline with LAPD:

4-10-25 - Sent email to CCWprocessing@lapd.online to get on the wait list. Wait time was 18-24 months at the time.

4-17-25 - Received email confirmation that I was officially on the wait list and confirmed expected timeline.

5-27-25 - Requested "Record Review" from DOJ. Just an extra step to see if I was missing anything on my end that I was forgetting.

5-30-25 - Received DOJ letter for Record Review.

10-15-25 - Emailed LAPD for an update on the wait list. Just wanted to see if anything had changed since the last update.

10-15-25 - Received response in the afternoon. Currently they were working on applications from 10/2024.

11-19-25 - Received email instructing me to proceed to Permitium to start the application process. Finished application, set appointment for 1-26-26 @ 07:45 AM and paid $60.21

11-20-25 - Went and submitted LiveScan $120.00 - LowBudget LiveScan & Fingerpringing, Long Beach - Markus

12-29-25 - Emailed LAPD to confirm appointment and address.

1-2-26 - Received email confirming time and date with new address: LAPD Northeast Station 3353 N San Fernando Road LA, CA 90065

1-20-26 - Went for day 1 of CCW course. Banguard Protective Services " Craig" 8605 S Crenshaw Blvd Inglewood, CA 90303 Paid $250.00 +$12 processing fee. Includes ammo for qualification.

1-21-26 - Day 2 of class. Half classroom and other half Range day. Paid $23.69 range fee at Insight.

1-22-26 - Picked up CCW Qualification form and certificate of CCW class completion.

1-23-26 - Received email from LAPD confirming appointment and documents to bring to appointment.

1-26-26 - Arrived early for appointment and IA detective took me in and went through the interview. She asked about my application and confirming everything on it. Told me next steps and what to expect.

1-26-26 - Application status updated to pending detective check.

1-28-26 - Detective text messaged/called references.

1-29-26 - Application status updated to "Pending Supervisor Review"

2-1-26 - Application updated to "Pending Captain Review"

2-4-26 - Application updates to "Pending Commander/Chief Review"

2-17-26 - Application updated to " Pending Final Review"

2-19-26 - Received text/email to submit final payment. Paid $222.39

2-19-26 - Application approved

2-27-26 - CCW mailed out with issue date of 3-1-26

3-2-26 - CCW Card received!

327 days total from start to finish.

$282.60 - LAPD w/ Permitium fees

$285.69 - Banguard CCW class

$120.00 - LiveScan

$688.29 - Total

Patience is key. The process is pretty straight forward and worth it IMO. LAPD gets a lot of hate, but I had a good experience with them and knew what to expect going into this. It helped that I was constantly checking in and seeing if any other appointments opened up. I have seen many get lucky and have their appointments bumped up closer. Some of the people I talked to at the interview also had the same luck of moving up in line and finding closer appointments. It seems like they are moving along faster. Is it worth it? Totally. Best of luck to you all who pursue this through LAPD.

rows.title: SFPD CCW permit timeline + process
rows.body:
27 days agoTL;DR: Submitted original application
7/6/2025. Took my time with process, received permit in mail
2/20/2026, about 7.5 months. Probably could've done this in <3 months - spent 1 month waiting on references, scheduled psych eval 2 months out, scheduled range day 2 months out.

Timeline:

7/6

submitted application

7/15

received call to schedule first appointment

7/23

first appointment, fingerprints

SFPD had some trouble reaching all references for a bit

8/23

received email to schedule psych eval
• 10/25 did asynchronous psych eval
• 10/27 did psych eval with psychiatrist
• 11/13 emailed to schedule course and live fire

1/31

did course + passed live-fire exam with Saber tactics

2/9

submitted paperwork to Permitium

2/20 received

CCW permit via mail

Costs:

Probably nearly $1000, $400+ for the class + live-fire day, $400+ for the psych eval, $144 for the application fee.

Other notes:

I was told go to with SFPD, not SF Sheriff's department - I believe sheriff's department only has one person to handle CCW applications. The live-fire portion is extremely difficult to fail for SFPD. The instructors said they've never seen anyone fail. Overall, smooth process, but pretty drawn out.

12/10/25 - Interview done. Super easy. Went over the application questions. Was reminded to upload my livescan and to watch out for an email for authorization to complete CCW course.

1/14/26 - Received email to be authorized to complete CCW firearm training course.

rows.title: LAPD CCW Timeline 2025
rows.body:
Hi all-

I have been reading this page for a while during the process of obtaining my CCW and found the timelines and discussions very helpful. Thus, I hope you don't mind if I provide my timeline as it might help folks going through the process:

January 5, 2025 - sent email to LAPD CCW Unit to be placed on waitlist. They responded a few days later and told me the timeline was averaging 15-16 months.

October 29, 2025 - CCW Unit sends email that they have reached my spot on the waitlist and provide a link to complete the application. I complete it the same day and schedule first available appointment which was March 30, 2026 for interview.

October 30, 2025 - Complete Live Scan

November 5, 2025 - While ensuring Live Scan was sent, I see that there is an option to reschedule interview and find an appointment December 2, 2025.

November 15-16 - Complete 16-hour training (at The Range LA which is a really really good class and not a waste of time at all!)

December 2, 2025 - Complete interview with LAPD and verify that all requirements are met.

December 3, 2025 - References called by LAPD

December 17, 2025 - CCW approved

December 23, 2025 - CCW in-hand

As a side note, I purchased another gun after completing the application (Staccato HD P4 CA Compliant) and asked at the interview if they could add it to the CCW. As I had qualified on it and it was registered to me, they were totally cool with it. I hear that other departments are not ok with adding outside of renewals, etc. but not an issue with the LAPD CCW Unit for me.

Good luck everyone with the process. Once you get the application, it seems to move pretty quick (at least in my experience).

Thanks again for this reddit. I find it very helpful/useful.

rows.title: SFPD CCW application process, timeline, breakdowns and rants
rows.body:
I went through a CCW permit process with SFPD some time late last year. Here is the timeline and some notes. AMA.

TLDR:

After 95 days, $960 and 22 hours of actual time spent I got my CCW card in hand in SF. Not the worst but could be half the cost and 25% less actual time if they got rid of the psych eval for most people or allowed to do the psych eval and firearm training right away. Definitely worth doing now if you have the money and can spend the time, the barrier to entry is not that high any more and it's not as tedious as, say, LA insane timelines or SJ 9x fees.

Timeline

Day 1: Application submitted, application fee $144. Very reasonable IMO.

Day 5 (4 days later): Got a call from SFPD and scheduled my appointment for an interview.

Day 9 (4 days later): Interview and Livescan. Livescan was done on site at SFPD, the cost was included in the initial fee. Took about 45 minutes in total.

Day 11 (2 days later): All my 3 references got a call.

Day 33 (24 days later): SFPD email asking for a psych evaluation, immediately scheduled the psych eval test from LEPS, fee $417.

Day 39 (6 days later): Online test proctored over Zoom. About 400 multiple choice questions, very similar to MPII plus work history questionnaire. Nothing interesting, completed in about 3h.

Day 39 (Same day): Later in the day got an email asking for an online psych interview, immediately scheduled.

Day 44 (5 days later): Psych eval interview over Zoom. Lasted all of 10 minutes, the interviewer just seemed to have gone through a checklist, he didn't have any questions about my test answers. Promised to submit the results directly to SFPD within 5 work days.

Day 52 (8 days later): Wrote an email asking whether SFPD have received the psych certificate. They indeed have had received it, the case was waiting for the unit supervisor's approval.

Day 53 (1 day later): Got an email requesting firearm training. Signed up for an online class with AAO same day.

Day 55 (2 days later): Completed the online portion of the class over 2 days and about 10 hours of continuous time. The website is a little buggy, but nothing beats an ability to complete it on your own time and not having to drive for an hour both ways. The fee was $199.

Day 57 (2 days later): Live fire portion completed, fee $200.

Day 59 (2 days later): Got the certificate over email and submitted it to SFPD.

Day 86 (27 days later): Got an email notification that permit has been issued.

Day 87 (1 day later): Permit issue date on card.

Day 95 (8 days later): Card received via USPS.

Money spent

Application Fee

$144

Psych Evaluation

$417

Online Firearm Class

$199

Live Fire Class

$200

Total

960

Total Time Spent (Active)

SFPD Interview

40 min

Online Psych Test

3 hours

Psych Interview

10 minutes

Online Firearm Class

10 hours

Live Firearm Class

8 hours

Total

21 hours 55 minutes

Rant:

The SFPD officer I interacted with was very polite, responsive and punctual. Overall the process is mostly reasonable, straightforward and not totally dragged out, except for several things:

- Requiring everyone to pass a LEO-level employment psych eval. This is a total overkill: if you don't have a history of mental issues and the SFPD officer interviewing you doesn't notice anything out of the ordinary this is completely unreasonable and a huge waste of time and money. Without that dumb detour the time required would decrease 30% and the expense would be cut almost in half. The time-to-permit would also be improved by 2-4 weeks.

- The psych eval cost itself is absolutely extortionate: there is one non-qualified proctor probably making $20/hour monitoring 12 of you and then a 10 minute 1-on-1 conversation with a qualified person. How is this $417 and not $50? SFPD allows for only 2 approved vendors, and their cost is exactly the same as well - aren't they supposed to charge "an actual cost"? A firearm class is 16 hours and can be found cheaper. I definitely sense cause for a lawsuit here.

- SFPD explicitly forbids to do the psych eval or firearm training before they clear you for the first and the second, in order. I don't understand the policy. If one is not worried about their background, why not allow them to get on with it? The whole process would have taken me 4-6 weeks, at most. Moreover, if I could just submit the certificates at the initial interview, I'd be done within 2-3 weeks with way less administrative hassle on the SFPD side as well. I honestly don't get it.

- A month-long wait after the firearm training certificate submission. By that point I've been already vetted by everyone, including the police chief: I was told that this happens after the initial interview, SFPD won't let you to proceed to psych eval if they find a reason to not issue you a permit. My case has been looked at from all possible angles. Issuing the permit at this point should be all but a formality and should not take more than a couple of days. Definitely not a month.

Other notes:

Make sure you choose the guns that go on your permit wisely: SFPD doesn't accept applications to modify listed guns right now (might change soon), so you are stuck with whatever you've chosen until you renew in 2 years. It goes without saying that one should try to put 3 handguns on your permit - what happens if one of them is out of commission or you want to sell it for whatever reason?

The caliber situation is unclear. There used to be a limitation on the calibers you could carry (https://www.sanfranciscopolice.org/sites/default/files/2023-04/SFPDCCWTrainingStandardForm20230403.pdf), but I can't find any mention of this limitation in any of the current documents.

I can definitely recommend AAO (https://aao-co.com/) for the firearm training class. You can take the classroom portion online, so you only need to come to the range once for 8 hours. The instructors were all professional, corteous and very nice. I'm not a new shooter and easily passed the qual, but the senior instructor spent some 1-1 time with me to show how I can be better - I really appreciated that. And you get to take shoot in the middle of a vineyard!

Instead of the completely wasteful psych eval I'd lengthen the live fire portion of firearm training. There were 15 "cadets" including me, 3 people adding new guns to their permit and one guy re-qualifying. If I were honestly evaluating everyone, no one but me would have passed:

Most people had no idea how to safely holster or draw their firearms from concealment.

Every single target but mine looked like they were shot with shotguns.

Several people had no clue how to properly grip their handgun.

Several people had no idea about the manual of their firearms. I was explaining basic handling to both my neighbors on the firing line.

The 4 basic rules were broken several times.

There were 3 very competent instructors, but there was no way they could teach everyone all the required skills in the 8 hours we had at the range. I'm legitimately scared that my fellow course attendees will be carrying now.

The other idea is to add a requirement for the CCW instructor to evaluate the psych state and mental abilities of cadets. They are spending 2 straight days with you, that is plenty of time to get a sense if you are relatively safe and competent. An instructor should just notify the PD if something looks off - and that should trigger a psych eval. But, I guess, none of the people making these decisions care even a little bit.

Links:

Application and info page: https://www.sanfranciscopolice.org/get-service/carrying-concealed-weapon-ccw-information-and-application

List of approved training vendors: https://permitium-downloads.s3.amazonaws.com/SFPD/SFPDccwTrainingProviders.pdf

Approved psych eval vendors:

- CORE Psychological Corporation (CORE): https://corepsychcorp.com,

- LEPS: applicants@lepsinc.com, scheduling at: https://app.acuityscheduling.com/schedule.php?owner=16336279&appointmentType=66015306

rows.title: Sacramento CCW Timeline
rows.body:
Thought I'd drop by and post my CCW timeline, fees and process for Sacramento County.

10/15; Initial application $302.97

11/12; Interview with Sheriff department, basically went over your application. Be truthful and don't forget anything like I did. I forgot to put I was stopped for blue headlights 2 years ago and the person interviewing me was grilling me for any "detainment" by law enforcement. (30 mins)

11/14; Live fingerprint scanning (20 mins) $137.50

12/6; Day 1 of the 16-hour course with Martin Mapa with AIMS Firearms Training in Elk Grove. Awesome instructor and true advocate for gun safety. We went over safety, rules and guidelines for CCW.

12/7; Day 2, outdoor range for shooting qualifications and holster safety. This is where I give big thumbs up to Martin. He went to each individual to make sure we were handling the gun safely out of holsters and fix any errors we had. Martin will take you out of your comfort zone and will push you to do better and correct your mistakes. We had some spare time, so we did move and shooting drills and ducking behind cover which was quite fun and educational. Shooting qualifications were straight forward and simple. I will definitely be back to progress my shooting skills! Submitted the certifications right after class. $300+$20 range fee

12/8; Received email that my CCW permit was ready for pick up the next day. $297.80

12/9; Picked up CCW.

Total Cost: $1,058.27 (this cost is before ammo, holsters, and belt)

All in all I think I got really lucky with my timeline and was able to finish within 2 months. Would've been sooner if my carry had arrived sooner but was stuck for 3 weeks due to some online computer error from Turner's side. Big shoutout to Martin at AIMS for the CCW course as it was quite helpful and the drills we did were really educational and fun!

rows.title: LAPD CCW Timeline and Cost
rows.body:
Here is a breakdown of the total time it took for me to obtain my CCW license along with the associated costs.
• 09/25/2024: Applied for CCW by sending an email to LAPD. I was told the current wait is over 1 year.
• 09/30/2025: Received an email instructing me to complete the application and schedule an interview. I schedule the interview for
10/10/2025 ($60)
• 10/09/2025: Did livescan at UPS (~$150)
• 10/10/2025: Went for interview at provided LAPD station.
• 11/15/2025: Signed up for firearms training. I would have done it sooner but I was getting my CZ ported and coated which took 4 weeks hence the delay in signing up. ($350)
• 12/07/2025: Completed firearms training.
• 12/08/2025: Got an email that my permit is approved and to pay the final fee. ($220)

Overall time took about 1 year and costed me ~$780.

UPDATE
12/19/2025: Received my permit in the mail!

rows.title: Riverside CCW renewal timeline
rows.body:
I know I like to be able to search for this information, so:

Renewal application submitted: 8/6/25

Uploaded training/qualification: 9/6/25

Notice of approval: 11/24/25

So about 3.5 months. I submitted my renewal application, uploaded my training certification, then just received my approval out of the blue. This was my first renewal so I wasn't sure if there would be any phone interview or anything else, but there wasn't.

I was also wondering if any of the new requirements that have taken effect since I was first approved might come into play (references, mental eval?) - but nope. Simple and easy. It's frustrating to sit around for 3 months wondering if you're really being processed or if your app got stuck in a black hole ​somewhere. But RSO CCW comes through again! Really glad I live in this county.

But for all you out of state applicants: RSO is slow and terrible, you should definitely apply to some other county up north. ; )

rows.title: LAPD CCW TIMELINE -NOVEMBER 2025
rows.body:
i recently got my final approval and currently waiting for my Card to arrive , here is a little breakdown of the process in my case .

-6/8/24 i submit my LAPD APPLICATION

-7/10/25 sent a email to LAPD CCW unit to check status of my application and i was told that there are roughly 200 people ahead of me

-9/8/25 Received a phone call from 213 number confirming that i own a firearm already and to proceed with a link sent to my email to create an account on Lapd permitium . ( keep in mind that i disable all my phone spam blocker just to get the call from LAPD & had to deal with the daily spam calls for a year .

-9/10/25 i printed the background check form from permitium and proceeded with background check CA,FBI, FIRE ( Cost $150)

-9/24/25 interview date that was given to me at 1 pm at Northridge division

-9/23/25 Received an email with interview location change to san fernando station near Glendale Ca

-9/24/25 arrived at the station around 12 and there were already 7 people ahead of me , at 1:30 Pm i got called to room full of Detectives to do my interview Detective went over my application and told me that my background result is not in yet but he did mention that he appetite that i mentioned everything about me and my background in my application. speeding tickets / AREST Report from 15 years go which was in result of fighting but case went from felony to misdemeanor and no weapon was used ( Fist Fight). and 2 vehicle accidents

-9/28/25 my application on permitium went from interview to pending background check result pending

-10/20/25 my background check result finally came in and my permitium changed from pending background check to Review

-10/24/25 Received an email saying do not proceed with training till further notice

-10/28/25 Application process on premitium changed from background check review to supervisor review

-10/30/25 changed from supervisor review to detective evaluation

-11/2/25 changed from detective evaluation to Chief review

-11/3/25 changed from Chief review to Firearm Training submissions pending

-11/4/25 Received an email that i could proceed with Firearm traning

-11/6/25 started my 16 hour training at Cobra tactical in santa clarita

-11/10/25 i completed my training and uploaded my training certificate on permitium site

-11/14/25 Permitium changed from firearm training submission to firearm training Issuance

-11/17/25 received a CALL which i missed and email saying congratulations i got approval on my permit and to make the final payment

-11/17/25 made the final payment of $222.39 on permitium site

-11/18/25 permitium changed to Permit approved

1-11/18/25 Received an email indicating my CCW CARD will be Mailed to the address in my application and as of now i don't need to contact LAPD for card pickup and this may take 4-8 weeks .

overall this was very very long process and somewhat stressful since i did have some stuff in my background. on my interview day i told The detective everything i could remember about my background and story behind it . just make sure you come clean and don't hide anything since your background will eventually show everything.

the total days before receiving my card roughly 520 days ,almost 1.5 year

rows.title: LAPD CCW TIMES IMPROVEMENT
rows.body:
Just wanted to post my experience so far. I know plenty of people are looking for timelines on getting their own CCW. I know I sure was. Made this damn account just to contribute lol.

TL;DR - 8 Months from Waitlist to Application. Chose the first available interview date for 2 months later. 10 months total from waitlist to interview date isn't bad.

I never applied because I knew the timelines were so horrible. But once I knew that LAPD was moving to Permitium, I figured I'd give it a shot.

I e-mailed in March of 2025 to get on the list and completely forgot about it.

I remembered in April and saw I never got a confirmation e-mail. So I e-mailed to verify. Got an answer the next day that yes I was on the list and they're just really busy.

I received my "go-ahead" and link to fill out the Application a few days ago. So for me, from waitlist to application was about 8 months.

I was also able to select an interview date in January. So less than a year total for waitlist to interview isn't bad. Especially since I've seen some people who got on the list 2-3 years ago just get their application dates a few months ago lol.

Get at it boys and girls.

rows.title: Non-Resident CCW Process: Orange County Edition - Full Timeline and Cost Breakdown
rows.body:
Received my Non-Resident CCW in the mail today from OCSD as a Florida resident. Filled out the application the day after the policy was announced and initial online kinks were worked out. Full disclosure: I was a previous CCW holder with OCSD while I was a CA resident, but Uncle Sam took me out of the state and turned me into Florida Man. This is possibly why my Non-Resident CCW license number is the same as my old CA Resident one, and I ended up paying the Renewal rate as opposed to a brand-new out-of-state initial applicant. Application was filled out via the CCW Pro website, fingerprinting was done by my local sheriff's department. Interview was done via Zoom call, authorized trainers list was given right after interview, classroom training was completed online via Ten-8 Tactical, and shooting qual was done at my local range. Shooting qual consisted of a safe loading/unloading demo followed by 8 shots each at 3, 5, and 7 yards on a B-27 target. Had a few delays on my own part, due to work and misplacing my physical CRPA Membership card and getting it replaced. I had initially sent in screenshots of my active membership page on the CRPA website and had no issue, but OCSD clarified just before final approval, saying they wanted pics of the physical card. Full timeline is as follows:

23APR25: Application filled

20MAY25: Application signed and submitted

27MAY25: Interview date set for 19NOV25

28AUG25: Interview auto-rescheduled to 02OCT25

22SEP25: Mailed off fingerprint cards and $93 money order for the Live Scan Fee

02OCT25: Interview complete, cleared for classroom training and shooting qual.

16OCT25: Online training complete, e-mailed OCSD and uploaded to CCW Pro

23OCT25: Shooting qual complete, emailed OCSD and uploaded to CCW Pro. OCSD emails response asking for picture of my physical CRPA Membership Card to finish all the paperwork, I can't find the damn thing and I call up CRPA for a replacement.

27OCT25: CRPA card arrives, I send and upload pictures right away.

28OCT25: CCW final approval e-mail comes in, $169 payment made via credit card on CCW Pro.

29OCT25: E-mail notification saying CCW permit is mailed off and on its way, says 3-7 business days for arrival.

03NOV25: CCW arrives in mailbox.

Full cost breakdown:

Live Scan Fee: $93 (paid via money order)

Fingerprint fee from local FL sheriff's department: $10 (cash only)

Online CCW class from Ten-8 Tactical: $175

Shooting Qual Fee at my local range: $26.83

Ammo Cost: $6 for 50 rounds of 9mm

License "Renewal" Fee: $163 + $6 credit card fee = $169. (Previous OC resident CCW holder.)

TOTAL: $479.83

Overall, I had a great experience with the folks at OCSD. Despite being as swamped as they are, everyone was super professional and friendly. Happy to answer any questions!

rows.title: LAPD CCW Timeline and Cost (Oct 2025)
rows.body:
Now that I am reaching the end of the process, it's time for my obligatory share of my experience with the CCW process for LAPD. I'll provide a timestamp for each of the steps and a tiny bit of color commentary:

2/14/24 - Emailed CCW Request to LAPD

Elapsed: 1 Year, 5 Months, 2 Days
7/16/25 - Missed phone call from LAPD. Per instructions, sent an email that I am still interested
7/20/25 - Phone Call w/ LAPD. Five minutes. Straightforward. Same questions as the application.
7/21/25 - Submitted application and uploaded documents to web portal. Used CA REAL ID and gave LADWP bill as proof of residence.

Application Fee: $60.21

7/21/25 - Received email that my Detective Appointment has been scheduled for
8/22/25**.** Ugh.
7/23/25 - Received email that there are earlier appointments available. Rescheduled for
7/28/25**.** Yay!
7/25/25 - Live Scan Appointment

Live Scan: $143.00

7/28/25 - Detective Interview. Five minutes. Long drive for me during the middle of the work day. Received a follow up email the same day telling me to book my training course.

I dragged my feet for two months because of personal travel. This "wasted" time was well within my control to minimize.
9/28/25 - Completed CCW 16hr Training Course. Positive experience with MedicUp Consulting. Uploaded the certification to the LAPD web portal.

Training Cost - $395.98

10/8/25 - Received email with approval from Chief of Police
10/9/25 - Received email that my permit has been issued and pickup scheduled for
11/12/25

10/23/25 - Received email that once final payment is received, CCW License will be mailed to my residence.

$222.39 - Final Payment

Now I am going to wait to see if this thing shows up in the mail. If it hasn't by 11/12, then I will go to the LAPD for my appointment date.

Total Time: 1 Year, 8 Months, 9 Days (617 Days)

Total Cost: $821.58 (excludes gas, ammo, and firearms)

rows.title: San Bernardino CCW timeline (4 months)
rows.body:
6/2/25 - Completed application on Permitium
6/18/25 - Background appointment. Informed of old traffic ticket. Hired lawyer & had it dismissed.
8/26/25 - Background completed
8/28/25 - Signed up for online CCW class
9/14/25 - Completed online class
9/28/25 - Completed range test
10/3/25 - Permit approved
10/10/25 - Received permit in mail

Took roughly four months. Had I not had the ticket to resolve, it would have been issued two months sooner. Total cost was $956, including $300 for the attorney to resolve the ticket. I also did my AZ CCW during this time. That took less than one month and cost $140.

I have to say, overall it went pretty quick. The people in the CCW office were pleasant to deal with. Although I ended up doing an online class because that was most convenient, SBSD offers an in person class that's under 200 bucks. I did the range qualification at Riverside Indoor Shooting Range, mainly because I have a membership there. Prior to this I lived in LA County, and that would have taken years. Glad I did it, and glad I can carry in my home state now.

If you're in San Bernardino and thinking of doing it, go ahead.

rows.title: LAPD CCW Timeline at 21 - 569 Days
rows.body:
Welp, after almost 19 months, I've finally obtained my CCW. LAPD was kind enough to let me get on the waitlist when I was 20.

Everyone I dealt with in the department was super kind and seemed eager to get the permit in my hands. The "interview" was very laid back. Of the five minutes I was there, the detective spent most of it talking about my job. She asked me so many questions about it - how I liked it, what the pay and benefits were like, and how flexible they were with hours, since I'm a college student. I work at In-N-Out, lol.

On the pickup day, there was a line around 4:15 PM, but it moved quickly. The detective who gave me my card was super chill and said I'm most likely the youngest LAPD CCW holder at the moment. He and a couple other officers told me congratulations as I walked out.

Timeline:

03/18/24 Called LAPD CCW dept and left a voicemail
03/21/24 Placed on waitlist, told 9-12 month wait

11/XX/24 Turned 21 (yippee!)
07/23/25 Appt date given by LAPD on the phone
07/25/25 Application submitted via Permitium ($60.21)
07/26/25 Livescan completed with A1 Livescan in Northridge ($120)
07/26/25 Livescan CA and FBI cleared
07/29/25 Livescan Firearms cleared
08/05/25 Interview with LAPD at the Northeast Station
08/22/25 References called (only three of the five)

08/23-24/25 16hr training with Nationwide CCW ($360)
08/24/25 Training certificate uploaded to Permitium
08/26/25 Status changed to "pending background"

09/03/25 Status changed to "pending supervisor review"

09/03/25 Status changed to "pending background" ???
09/08/25 Status changed to "pending supervisor review"

09/09/25 Status changed to "pending captain review"

09/11/25 Status changed to "pending commander/chief review"

09/16/25 CCW Approved!
09/25/25 Final payment made in Permitium ($222.29)
10/08/25 CCW card picked up at LAPD HQ

Total: $762.60 with Permitium fees. Total excluding gas, ammo, parking, food, etc.

I should've picked up my card on September 10
th, but for some reason, my application got mixed up with someone else's and somehow his information ended up on my application. What screwed it up the most was changing my application to say I'm married (I'm not). Of course, you're supposed to put your spouse down as a reference if you're married, which caused my application to keep bouncing back and forth between "pending background" and "pending supervisor review" for about two weeks. Early in the morning on 9/8, a very nice detective called me and asked why I didn't list my wife on my application. I was very confused. I said I wasn't married, and she corrected it. She told me that my record is squeaky clean and that I'll have no issue getting approved, BUT... I wouldn't be able to make the 9/10 pickup date; I would have to wait until 10/8. I was really annoyed because I made friends with a couple of guys in my CCW class, and they got their approvals the week before and were able to pick up on the 10th :/

rows.title: Sac County CCW Timeline 2025
rows.body:
Timeline of my experience getting CCW in Sac County:

- June 16, 2025: Initial application. Paid $55 then, it has now jumped up to $288

- September 8, 2025: Interview with Sheriff's office, interview lasted about 20-30 mins and was cleared about 45 minutes or so after that. Did LiveScan 2 days after interview. LiveScan cost was roughly $140

- September 15, 2025: Received LiveScan clearance and registered for CCW Course. Paid $300

- October 5, 2025: Submitted course completion certificate and instructor credentials to Permitium

- October 6, 2025: Cleared all documents and received green light for pick up. Paid $190 for finalizing fee.

- October 7, 2025: Stare at CCW and cry

Wishing everyone the best of luck with their process!

rows.title: LAPD CCW Renewal Timeline
rows.body:
Was wondering if anyone here has gone through the LAPD CCW renewal process lately? How long did it take them to get back to you when you sent out the initial email? How about the whole process itself?

Earlier this year, i had noticed the website said you should contact them 90 days before your permit expires. But a few weeks ago when i checked the site, it said to contact them 60 days prior, and now they have appeared to have revised it back to 90 days, which is a bit strange.

It's been crickets for me since i sent out the email, so I'm wondering how long this will take.

rows.title: CCW Renewal Timeline - Riverside County (39 Days)
rows.body:
8/7/25 - Submitted required docs and training cert ($85.26+$175 for training)
8/22/25 - Permit expired. oof
9/4/25 - Called the permit office to check on my app and see if they had what they needed, and the deputy I spoke to pulled it from the stack to review it and said he would get back to me.
9/11/25 - Said deputy called me and said that he confirmed my app was complete and that he submitted it for approval the day prior.
9/15/25 - Got a call that the permit was approved and ready for pickup. (Might have a small fee at pickup, but idk)

A few things to note.

TAKE YOUR TRAINING FIRST. RivCo will let you submit your training with your initial application and this will speed things up by a few weeks since they won't have to request it and than get back to it later.

Wait a few weeks and call to check on your permit, it goes in the stack with new apps and SB2 really slowed down the process. Since it was a renewal and my application was complete, he assigned it to himself and moved up the processing. My friend submitted a month prior and it was just approved today.

RivCo is up to ~60K permits now and they are swamped between the renewals, non-res applications, and some recent departures in the Palm Desert office that need to be filled. They will let you start the process 6 months prior to get ahead of it, so don't be a dumbass like me and do it 3 weeks before it expires.

rows.title: Santa Clara CCW Received
rows.body:
Hey folks, just picked up my CCW from Santa Clara County and thought I'd post a breakdown of the timeline & costs:

Application submitted: 12/17/24, initial interview date: 12/9/25 - $345

(was able to reschedule the interview sooner, I checked every other day)

Initial interview: 4/8/25, was told that the expected wait would be about 30 days

Follow-up email sent to office: 6/10/25: received response same-day saying I should receive an email soon

Psych Testing Email: 6/11/25 - $415

Psych Test Pt. 1 (written test): 7/2/25

Psych Test Pt. 2 (interview): 7/14/25

References Contacted: 7/18/25- 8/8/25 - Two of my references talked w/ the department that week, the third didn't pick up at all, and I had to provide a new reference. The new reference was able to get through to the department and talk on
8/8/25.

Livescan Go-ahead Email: 7/22/25

Livescan Appointment: 7/24/25 - $113 - FBI / DOJ cleared 7/24, Firearms cleared 7/26

Training Email: 7/29/25, scheduled for 8/30 with Saber Tactics - $399

Training Class: 8/30/25, 8 AM - 12 PM, Los Altos R&G club

Training Certs Received & Uploaded: 9/2/25, proficiency form printed, signed, and scanned

Pickup Scheduling Email: 9/4/25, appt. scheduled for 9/12

Pickup: 9/12/25, received permit in hand

Total Cost: $1,272

Time from Initial App: 269 days (8 mo, 29 days)

Time from Interview: 157 days (5 mo, 7 days)

Takeaways

The process seems to have sped up a lot now compared to a year or two ago - most of the initial glut of applications seem to have been processed

The biggest bottleneck is the initial interview - almost 41% of the "wait" for the permit was just the first three-ish months that I was waiting for my initial interview

The department is pretty responsive to emails and phone calls; if things go past the expected time line (e.g. my psych eval was a month late), contact them and they will be pretty helpful.

The psych eval delay seems to be more on the side of LEPS than the various IAs - they have very limited slots for both the initial test & the live interview

All the interactions I had with the department were very pleasant and professional

The officer I interacted with during both the interview and pickup was quite happy about the whole thing, and congratulated me on legally exercising my right

After the psych eval, everything else went pretty quick

I waited 7 days after the psych eval to get the Livescan email

I got the training email 7 days after the Livescan email

I was "approved" 2 days after submitting my training cert

I picked up 8 days after approval

I strongly recommend Saber Tactics for training

Being able to do 12 of the 16 hours online is super convenient

The instruction about drawing from concealment at the range was great

The whole qualification process is super well organized

The instructors are great guys and were more than willing to stay and answer questions

Santa Clara Qual is harder than San Mateo, but still really easy

rows.title: My CCW Timeline (Alameda County, CA)
rows.body:
I done did it boys. Picked up my permit this morning after 541 days.

March 2024

On March 17, 2024, I applied for my CCW with the Alameda County Sheriff's Office through Permitium at a cost of $90.

May 2025

On May 9, 2025, I received the Live Scan form and completed the fingerprinting on May 12 for $100.

On May 16, ACSO confirmed receipt of my Live Scan results but flagged that my driver's license in Permitium was expired. I updated the information, replied to their email, and received acknowledgment that an in-person interview would be scheduled soon.

On May 21, I received an interview scheduling request and replied within an hour; available dates were May 27–29 and the week of June 6.  My interview was scheduled for May 28.

On May 28, Interviewed at ACSO Dublin office. All of my references were contacted. I was told to follow up after two months if no updates were received by then.

July 2025

On July 8, I received an email to schedule my psychological evaluation. I booked the first available date: August 7 at 9:45 AM, at a cost of $419.97.

August 2025

On August 7, I attended the psychological evaluation. The exam included a short memory test (Milk – Secluded – Before) and questions about personality traits, cannabis history, use of force, and firearm use. Later that afternoon, ACSO cleared me to proceed with range qualification, which I scheduled with LEO Defensive Concepts for August 23–24 at a cost of $425.

On August 23-24, I attended the LEO Defensive Concepts 16 hour CCW training in Livermore.  I picked this training because they recommend IWB holsters for CCW. This was the only course I found that allowed IWB, all the others I found required OWB.

ACSO Qualification Requirements:Applicants must complete a live-fire qualification consisting of two 5-shot groups fired at each distance: 2, 5, 7, 15, and 25 yards. Scoring is based on hits within the designated scoring zones, and a minimum passing score of 175 is required.

I passed with both of my firearms.

P365x with EPS Carry scored 226/250 (90%)

TWC9 with irons scored 224/250 (89%)

EPS didn't arrive in time for qual day.

I submitted my certification to Permitium and notified Claudia at 6:06 PM.

On August 25, I received an email from Claudia that my license was ready for pickup.  I scheduled the first available calendly date on September 9.

September 2025

On September 5, I received an appointment/payment reminder from Claudia and, when I logged into Permitium, I was surprised to see another $100 fee due. I paid the balance the same day.

On September 9, 2025, I picked up my CCW license.

TLDR

541 days from application to license

Initial Permitium Application Fee: $90.00

Live Scan (Fingerprinting): ~$100.00 (forgot to document actual cost)

Psychological Evaluation: $419.97

Training & Range Qualification (LEO Defensive Concepts): $425.00

Permitium Final Balance: $100.00

Total Cost: $1,134.97

rows.title: Riverside CCW Renewal Timeline – Start to Finish
rows.body:
Hey everyone! I wanted to share a quick breakdown of my CCW renewal process with the Riverside County Sheriff's Office, in case it helps anyone else going through it:

06/16/2025 – Submitted my CCW renewal application through the Riverside Sheriff's Office.
06/29/2025 – Completed the required 8-hour renewal course and qualification shoot at Riverside Indoor Shooting Range.
07/10/2025 – Uploaded my training certificate and qualification documents. I held off a bit because I had purchased a new pistol that I wanted added to my permit. After the 10-day waiting period, I re-qualified with the new firearm and submitted everything.
09/09/2025 – Received a call, text, and email confirming that my renewal was approved and ready for pickup!

I had been checking Permitium every few days throughout the process, and it consistently showed "Assigning to Processor" until the final approval.

Hope this helps anyone else navigating the process!

rows.title: Contra Costa CCW experience
rows.body:
I am in the process of a Contra Costa CCW. Here's the timeline and experience for those wanting the most up-to-date situation:

August 20, 2025 Applied online

August 21, 2025 Electronic notification to get a live scan done within 30 days.

August 22, 2025 Live scan done at a local UPS

August 28, 2025 Electronic notification to schedule the interview in Martinez. I scheduled for earliest opening. -interviews every Wednesday with limited slots per website-

September 2, 2025 My references were all called at approximately 10am. They told me it was a short 5 minute phone call with verification of how long they have known me and other yes or no answers.

September 3, 2025 Interviewed in person for 5 minutes at 8:00am. Passed.

Setting : 1 on 1 sitting on folding chairs while using a plastic foldable table for the interview with Deputy. He wore white dress shirt, black slacks, a tie, dress shoes. I wore the same. others interviewing for CCW were more casual, wore street casual hoodie and shorts

Questions asked were rapid fire yes or no (but he would ask a quick question if you said yes to anything): "May I verify your ID?" "Is this address current?" "Ever applied for CCW out of state?" "Who do you live with? In a relationship?" "Ever been married?" "Ever done drugs? How long ago?" "History of alcoholism?" "Ever been in a car accident in last 5 years? Explain." "Road rage?" "Diagnosed with mental health in your past? Prescribed medications for anxiety, depression, etc?" "Do you have any restraining orders against you?" "Kids accessing your home regularly?" "History of domestic violence?" "Police called on you?" "You called police on neighbors?" "Ever been arrested?" "Have you visited anyone in Jail before?"

-Finishing up: I asked him about timeline and what's next. He said I shouldn't have to wait for an all-clear to go and take a CCW 16 hour course. He said based on my answers today, I passed. He said the backlog is no longer with the application process. Now the backlog is with the firearm shooting qualification hosted by the Sheriffs office. Currently backlogged till January 2026. In the meantime, he recommended to take a CCW course.

Interesting remarks:

Deputy told me that the process is definitely faster. I asked how they made it faster. He simply said "they make us work harder." He remarked having done 1,000 applications by himself since December of 2024.

Deputy was impressed how fast I was processed. He saw it had only been two weeks since my application started.

Another guy I met at the building was coming to pickup his CCW card. Through small talk he told me has been waiting for this CCW card for two years. He is military and used military station orders or something like that.

I am sharing this to provide the most up to date Contra costa CCW info I have right now. I literally just left this interview 1 hour prior to posting here. Let me know if you have questions.

UPDATE to Reddit Post

September 4, 2025 Electronic Approval of CCW application; contingent on taking 16 hr CCW classes and passing shooting qualification.

Email says "Failure to complete your training 60 days from the date of receiving the request for training will result in the withdrawal of the CCW Application, as will inactivity on the application." And also says, "YOU MUST COMPLETE THE TRAINING FIRST AND UPLOAD THE PROOF OF TRAINING DOCUMENT AND THE TRAINING CERTIFICATE BEFORE SCHEDULING THE RANGE QUALIFICATION." (copied and pasted from the email. Yes, it was in all caps in the email.

September 8, 2025 Uploaded my completed CCW Class certificates and proof of training forms.

September 9, 2025 Received Electronic approval link to schedule shooting test with CCCSO. Signed up for nearest date available: December 20, 2025. at time of writing, next nearest available date after Dec 20 was January 10.

December 20, 2025 Passed the live fire shooting exam at the CoCo Sheriff's Marsh Creek Shooting Range. Drill was simple; 6 shots from 5 yards, 6 shots from 7 yards, and 6 shots from 10 yards. All timed. You fire from a static line. The actual shooting test takes less than 2 minutes. Most of the time spent was safety briefing and filling out a quick form about your weapon, address, etc. Bringing your own weapon, holster, PPE, drivers license, and ammunition is mandatory. Total time at the range: 30 minutes.

December 22, 2025 Received Email and voicemail confirmation of CCW application acceptance. Paid the final CCW application fee and picked the earliest available day for CCW permit pickup: December 31, 2025. Pickups are every Wednesday and Thursday per website.

December 23, 2025 SURPRISE!Received an email stating the following, "Due to the extended wait time for permit pick-up appointments and holiday closures, as a courtesy I have decided to print your permit prior to your appointment. Your CCW card is ready for pick up. You may pick up today between 1:30pm-4:30pm, tomorrow between 8am-12pm, or Friday between 8am-4:30pm. Next week Monday-Friday we are open 8am-4:30pm. We will also be closing early on New Year's Eve but are open from 8am-12:30pm. We will be closed for Christmas and New Years Day. If you would like, you may also send someone to pick up your permit for you if that is easier so long as they can provide your name and a valid ID. If you have any questions you may respond to this email." Picked up my CCW permit card. Easy in and out. Walk in the front door with your Drivers license and the permit will be in in your hand within seconds. Like that, it's officially done! ✅ Total time: 125 days from online application till pickup. August 20 to December 23, 2025.

GOOD LUCK to everyone interested in getting their CCW. If you have any questions, please feel free to reply!

rows.title: Torrance PD CCW Timeline and Cost (After Permitium Implementation)
rows.body:
This Timeline and cost was for my initial CCW.

24APR2025 - I applied online through Permitium portal (on Torrance PD website) Initial application cost was $50

22JUL2025 - Emailed Torrance CCW email asking for an update on my application. (No Response)

01AUG2025 - Received a phone call from the CCW detective asking to set my appointment for my interview (I think due to my email)

07AUG2025 (8:45AM)- I had my interview and all they asked was questions to confirm the information I had on my application he took my picture and my fingerprint. (9:30AM) I took my livescan off site. Cost was $138 (12:00pm) CCW Detective called all my references and asked simple questions like [if he part of gang?, ETC]

09AUG2025 - Took my first 8 hour CCW class at ELITE CCW (Great team would recommend) kind of far but I would definitely drive to take it there again. Cost $400 for 16 hour class. Worth the money.

16AUG2025 - Took my second 8 hour CCW class to complete the course and receive my certificate. I submitted all the documents same day to the portal.

18AUG2025 - Texted the CCW detective to inform him that I submitted my documents and if there is anything else that I need to submit. He said no and accepted my application for my permit on the spot. I paid a final $50 and set a date to pick up my CCW.

20AUG2025 - This is the set day I have to pick up my ccw.

Total Length: A little less than 4 months Total Cost: $638 (could be less with different course and different livescan location)

Overall Torrance PD did a great job and CCW Detective Rich Gabel was on top of everything if you don't plan on moving soon I would definitely recommend going the Torrance PD Route.

rows.title: Notes and Tips
rows.body:
Total time: 679 days from application submission to pickup

Checking Permitium regularly helped me move my interview from May 2024 to January 2024

The longest delay was from interview to P2T: almost 10 months. This was insanely long and consider it an outlier because they were likely prioritizing the paper applicants before working on the permitium applicants.

While not explicitly advised, some people said to complete training ahead of time so you can submit documents as soon as you receive the P2T. It might be helpful if your schedule is busy and cannot dedicate 16 hours for a weekend of training within a spontaneous 60 day window. (LASD requires training to be done within the 60 day window of the P2T email). YMMV, so proceed at your own caution as LASD does not explicitly advise this, but I can confirm it was a positive experience for me. MEDIC UP's training documents are good for a year I believe.

Be patient. It might be very frustrating waiting for so long (and rightfully so), but always keep it professional. LASD was professional and respectful throughout the process.

Good luck to any LASD applicants out there. Be safe!

rows.title: LASD CCW Under 2 Years Timeline - Change of Address Halfway Through - Sep '23 to Jul '25
rows.body:
Thank you all for keeping the group sane throughout the past few years with the long wait lines. Here's my official timeline, and I hope it helps someone else who is in the process still. One thing I'd like to emphasize: I triple checked everything to not add ANY hurdles on my end as it makes their lives harder and in return mine miserable.

September 2023 - Application sent online with all the appropriate documentation they requested.

December 2023 - Livescan submitted. There was no communication on their part and I had to do my own research and take that step.

Late December 2023 - A few days after submitting the livescan work, I was given an early July 2024 interview date. I obsessively checked the "reschedule" page on my phone multiple times a day until I found a sudden early January 2024 availability and immediately took it. About a week prior to my interview appointment, I received a text and email that my interview had been pushed 6 months back to July of 2024 (this happened to most if not all applicants, so technically I was still ahead) as they wanted to prioritize and work on older paper-submitted applications. Those people had been waiting for over two years already at that point.

July 2024 - I received a call from a Deputy who conducted the interview over the phone and made sure all of my information had been correct. I reported a potential change of address that was yet to happen and asked for his advice (still under LASD jurisdiction). I had to submit new proof of residency documentation which took me an additional week or so to receive new bills and ID. I told the Deputy that I wouldn't quote him but wanted an approximate time range of when I'd hear back - he said "most likely 3 months if all goes well, that's been the pattern." All went well, but the timeline did not.

I emailed twice and got boiler template responses. I called the dedicated number and was told they're doing what they can but are very limited on staff and have thousands of applicants - long story short: just wait and we'll reach out when we have an update.

Reddit posts started appearing about the delay in sending us to training, so it turned out to be a new standard instead of the older 3-months-long one.

May 2025 - I received permission to take training classes. I knew I could take training beforehand as it stays usable up to a year from taking it, but I'm not a lawyer and I did not want to gamble the training fees and time and have to do it twice if any of that was incorrect or the laws suddenly changed.

Late May 2025 - I completed the 16-hour two-day training, and a week later had the certification submitted.

A few days ago (Early July 2025) I received a call, text, and email about being approved and to schedule a pick up date. The dates were all about 3-4 weeks ahead, but luckily I found one timeslot that was 6 days away and took it, bringing my total LASD CCW process to one year and 10 and a half months.

Best of luck to you and don't give up! Stay safe out there.

About a week prior to my interview appointment, I received a text and email that my interview had been pushed 6 months back to July of 2024 (this happened to most if not all applicants, so technically I was still ahead) as they wanted to prioritize and work on older paper-submitted applications. Those people had been waiting for over two years already at that point.

July 2024 - I received a call from a Deputy who conducted the interview over the phone and made sure all of my information had been correct. I reported a potential change of address that was yet to happen and asked for his advice (still under LASD jurisdiction). I had to submit new proof of residency documentation which took me an additional week or so to receive new bills and ID. I told the Deputy that I wouldn't quote him but wanted an approximate time range of when I'd hear back - he said "most likely 3 months if all goes well, that's been the pattern." All went well, but the timeline did not.

I emailed twice and got boiler template responses. I called the dedicated number and was told they're doing what they can but are very limited on staff and have thousands of applicants - long story short: just wait and we'll reach out when we have an update.

Reddit posts started appearing about the delay in sending us to training, so it turned out to be a new standard instead of the older 3-months-long one.

May 2025 - I received permission to take training classes. I knew I could take training beforehand as it stays usable up to a year from taking it, but I'm not a lawyer and I did not want to gamble the training fees and time and have to do it twice if any of that was incorrect or the laws suddenly changed.

Late May 2025 - I completed the 16-hour two-day training, and a week later had the certification submitted.

A few days ago (Early July 2025) I received a call, text, and email about being approved and to schedule a pick up date. The dates were all about 3-4 weeks ahead, but luckily I found one timeslot that was 6 days away and took it, bringing my total LASD CCW process to one year and 10 and a half months.

Best of luck to you and don't give up! Stay safe out there.

rows.title: CA Non-Resident CCW (Fresno County) Timeline / Process - 34 Days
rows.body:
CA Non-Resident CCW (Fresno County) Timeline: Total days from application submission to CCW in mailbox - 34 days
05/27/2025 - Submitted Online Application
05/29/2025 - All 3 references were called
05/29/2025 - Sheriff's office called my phone to request the following: upload passport photo to application and mail out 2 fingerprint cards (Also asked if I would have training submitted before interview)
06/01/2025 - Submitted 16 Hour Non-Resident CCW Online Training Certificate
06/02/2025 - USPS Priority Mail Parcel with fingerprint cards received by Fresno County Sheriff's office
06/03/2025 - CCW Interview with Sheriff's Department (Facetime call)
06/18/2025 - Permit Approved Email Received
06/18/2025 - Paid final part of Application Fee
06/25/2025 - Permit Issued
06/30/2025 - Permit Received in mail (Charleston South Carolina area for distance context)

Cost Summary (Final Total: $233.03): $129.29 - Initial Application Fee $0.00 - Fingerprinting Fee (Local PD did not charge me a fee, but most people will probably have to pay for this average $10.00 at most PDs) $10.40 - USPS Priority Mail Parcel (For submitting fingerprint cards) $93.34 - Final Permit Issuance Fee ($92.00 Permit Fee + $3.34 Credit Card Fee (there was no option that would allow you pay with a method other than credit card to avoid the additional fee))

Notes from what I learned during the process:

Fresno County Sheriff's office will allow you to do your qualification live fire with an NRA Instructor local to you regardless of if they are a CA DOJ Approved CCW Instructor (must have the following NRA Instructor Certifications: Basic Pistol, Personal Protection Inside The Home, & Personal Protection Outside The Home)

Fresno County allows up to 6 pistols on the permit at a time. (From my research they seemed to be one of the more 2A friendly counties so I applied through them, although you could go through a county like Orange county where there is no limit, although I did not anticipate planning to bring more than 6 pistols to California, so I chose Fresno as it was more than the average limit which is 3…)

Notes about being eligible via GOA Membership: At the time of writing this you must be covered under a court injunction to apply, to qualify I chose to become a member of GOA. NOTE: GOA membership receipt is not enough to prove membership!!! You either need a letter on their letterhead confirming you are a member or your membership card. I ended up calling and submitting a ticket to GOA to obtain the necessary proof, when I called they emailed me a letter same day I could use for proof. The ticket i opened resulted in them emailing me a digital membership card about 3 weeks after and i got a letter the same day as my permit with a physical GOA membership card ie 34 days after submitting the ticket stating they expedited my card and I may still receive another one…

tl;dr: If you sign up for GOA and need expedited proof of membership, CALL THEM, do not open a ticket as it is exceptionally faster to get a letter from them than it is to get an expedited digital membership card… (2hrs vs. 3 weeks)

Intent to visit California within 12 months notes: Currently to apply you must state on the record that you have intent to visit the county you are applying to within the next 12 months. They will ask you what you plan to do on your visit during your interview so be prepared to answer that. I genuinely do intend to be in Fresno County within the next 12 months so i had a pretty solid answer to the question, but I would probably recommend making sure the justification you give makes the respective county the destination of your visit as they might try to make you apply to the destination county if your justification is something like "I plan to stop for gas while passing through on the interstate"… With that being said I would not advise lying on your justification and since we have no idea what will be requested on these non-resident permit renewals, I would definitely make sure to actually make a trip that is documentable to the county of issuance within the validity time of the permit, sorta as a CYA thing…

If anyone has questions about the process feel free to ask and I'll do my best to answer them… Fresno County Sheriff's Office did tell me they are still working out the process so there will probably be improvements and that I was like the 5th person to apply as a non-resident to their county…

rows.title: San Diego County CCW Renewal Timeline And Process 2025
rows.body:
Just finished my ccw renewal in San Diego county and wanted to share the process and time it took from the point of application to actually receiving the license. Also wanted to show how the ID has changed. The yellow is my old ID and the blue is the new one.

TLDR: It took me 34 days from hitting the submit button to having the permit in my had. If you include the CCW class lead time, it took 60 days.

TIMELINE AND FEES

For the renewal, things were a little different than my initial application. I was not required to go down to the SD sheriffs office, however, I needed to have my 8 hour CCW renewal course re-certification before applying online. I reserved my class at Poway Gun range on April 15
th, and the earliest I was able to get in was for May 11
th. The class was $299. Took the class and passed no problem, got my certificate/Issuance letter, and went home to submit it. From that point (May 11
th) it took until June 5
th for the renewal to update, and I got an email saying payment was required. The cost for me was $79.39. I think I paid the fee the same day, but it might have been the next day. From the time of payment submission (June 5
th) it took until June 14
th for the permit to show up in the mail.

REQUIRED DOCUMENTS AND INFORMATION

Once you've made your account on the new CCW Pro sheriffs website, you will need to fill out several pages of stuff.

Personal Info - Name, date of birth, phone number, SSN, that kind of thing.

ID Info - Drivers license and any restrictions.

Address - Where you live

Appearance - Your physical characteristics, Height, Weight, Eye color, etc-

Employment and Weapons - Where do you work, what is their address and phone number? What Guns will be on your permit. This section is important. You can only add Firearms you've qualified with in the CCW class to your permit.

6 .Qualifying Questions - This section is 21 questions long, and is to see if you are prohibited from getting a CCW.

7. Upload Documents - This section is all the required documents you will need to submit photocopies of before you can submit the permit renewal. There are several combinations of documents that can get you through, but here is what I used.

Drivers License front and back

CCW Safety Course certificate

CCW Safety Course Issuance Letter

Proof of residency - I used my most recent Electric bill, and a bank statement

Supporting documentation - I used two pay stubs, my work ID, and my HVAC certification card.

In my opinion, the more things you can throw at them to prove you are who you say, the better. You are required to have at least one recurring city bill like an electric or water bill, but two would be better. I used two pay stubs and a bank statement instead of the second city bill. I'm pretty sure a mortgage or rent bill would work for the second recurring city bill, but don't quote me.

After all that, you will fill out a few more short things, and hit submit.

I think that covers the whole process for me, if I'm missing anything or got something wrong, let me know and I will correct it.

rows.title: Contra Costa CCW Timeline - in progress
rows.body:
Seems like Contra Costa is moving a little faster these days, when i applied i assumed it would be 12-18months.

December 28, 2024 - applied for CCW $80

April 2, 2025 - request for Live Scan

April 3 - Live Scan complete $130

April 19 - request for in-person interview

April 23 - in-person interview completed

April 24 - references called (only 2/3 were called)

April 28 - request for 16hr training

May 24 - pistol basics training (8hr - $250)

May 24 - submitted document of competed of 8hr course

May 27 - request for qualification with sheriff

May 30 - CCW class (8hr -$250)

May 30 - submitted remaining documents of completed training

July 19 - Shooting Qualification

July 22 - Approved and paid final dues and booked pickup appointment ($100)

Interview: In person interview is only held on Wednesdays and took about 5 minutes total after sitting down.

Interview Questions: Have you been involved in domestic violence? Have you been contacted by the police as a suspect or victim? Have you ever had a negligent discharge with a firearm? Do you use or have you ever used illegal substances? Have you been in a fight? How is your decision making? How do you handle stress?

Pistol modifications: I got mixed responses on pistol modifications from here and my LGS so I reached out to the sheriff and this was their response:

"Our policy is currently under revision, but our modification policy is: GENERAL. The alteration of a weapon approved for a CCW is prohibited.

This includes the addition of equipment, alteration of trigger pull, trimming of springs, honing or filing of mechanical parts or any similar or related alteration.

The addition of flashlights, laser sights, expanded capacity or extended magazines, or similar modifications, are prohibited and shall constitute an alteration.

The alteration of a weapon shall be cause for the immediate seizure of the weapon and revocation of the license."

I doubled down to ask specifically about changing the grip modules on the Sig P365 to other OEM grips modules like the XL or XMacro and was informed: "Currently only grip tape and stippling are permissible"

So approved modifications are: Iron sights, stippling, and grip tape.

rows.title: 2025 CCW Timeline for Orange County
rows.body:
Summited my application
11/14/24

- Initial Interview Date: 06/29/25.

- Tried several times to reschedule the interview to an earlier date, their website is still a work in progress and it would not allow me to reschedule on any of the open slots. Tried on several different browsers to no avail.

- Assigned an analyst on
05/06/25 and interview moved up to
05/28/25.

- Completed Livescan on
05/19/2025

- Interview conducted at 320 N Flower on
05/28/25. They called my references the same day probably an hour after my interview was completed.

- 2-Day training with Firearms Training Associates in Eastvale, CA on 05/31 - 06/01

- Issue with a DROS on one of my handguns delayed processing, had to reach out to the DOJ by phone to get a serial number corrected in their system. It took 2 weeks to reach someone but once I did, the correction took less than a day.

- Payment completed via phone and waiting for license as of
06/14/25.

Overall time was 212 days. Probably would have been around 190-200 days if not for the delays due to the DROS issue.

Total Cost: $123 Livescan ($30 rolling, $32 DOJ, $17 FBI, $44 Firearms) + $310 Training ($260 for the class and $25 each for additional handguns) + $169 for the Licence. = $602

If you want to nitpick the details and factor in things like a pistol (if you don't own one already), gas, ammo, or insurance a CCW here can easily cost $1-1.5k+, pretty absurd imo.

Some things to be aware of. OCSD's CCW Pro website is still a work in progress. Apparently some people were lost in the migration period from whatever old system they used so processing took longer than usual. Additionally, they are planning on adding credit card payments to the website in the future so hopefully that will streamline payment a bit.

Apparently some people were lost in the migration period from whatever old system they used so processing took longer than usual. Additionally, they are planning on adding credit card payments to the website in the future so hopefully that will streamline payment a bit.

rows.title: LASD CCW Timeline June 2025
rows.body:
My CCW journey has been harrowing, and I received great comfort throughout this time of tribulation from reading various CCW threads in r/CAguns so I wanted to give back with the hope that it will help others during their quest. 1 year and 10 months.
08/19/2023 Application submitted
10/18/2023 Advised by CCW Unit that my Livescan was rejected due to poor quality. I immediately redid Livescan at a different vendor and uploaded it
10/19/2023.
12/14/2023 Was notified that telephonic interview appointment that was scheduled for
03/05/2024 was rescheduled to
09/05/2024

09/05/2024 Interview appointment via phone completed, officer advised me I would receive an email by end of December 2024 to proceed with training assuming my application was approved. I was told not to do the training until advised to do so.
01/28/2025 Emailed CCW Unit asking when I should expect email to proceed with training, received a generic response the same day "Your file and live scan returns are pending review, we will contact you once completed."

04/29/2025 At this point I had just about given up except for reading CCW threads in here kept me breathing.
04/30/2025 Received email to proceed with training.
05/08/2025 Completed training (I ponied up for private training to get it done asap) and uploaded completion and proficiency forms same day.
06/06/2025 Emailed CCW asking if they tried contacting me since I missed a few calls, I received a generic response the same day "You will be contacted when your file has been approved, and we are ready to issue your license."

06/13/2025 Received the call 4:30pm on the nose (the number wasn't restricted, and it wasn't a person, rather a robo call) advising me my CCW application is approved and to goto Permitium to pay final fee and schedule pick up. I paid fee immediately and scheduled a pick up date. I want to note that the call came from a number that the CCW Unit had been previously using to send me update texts, so I had saved that number as CCW Unit Text. I obviously recognized it when I got the call today. I also received a text and an email in addition to the call. I'm just pointing this out, it is possible others may not receive their "call" this same way.
06/18/2025 Pick up date

rows.title: El Dorado County CCW timeline!
rows.body:
3-29 - CCW Application submitted on Permitium, appointment scheduled.

4-4 - Appointment day. Fingerprints/livescan complete. They just asked a few questions required by law.

4-26/27 Took the 16 hour course over the weekend. Live fire standards were ridiculously easy (30 rounds per gun at 5 or so yards lol). After getting home, I scanned the certificate and submitted it to Permitium.

4-28 CCW Permit approved and ready for pickup!

Overall the process was quite easy and I appreciate the speed at which the EDCS CCW Unit processed everything. Longest thing I waited for was the 16 hour course.

rows.title: Contra Costa County CCW Timeline
rows.body:
After expecting the process to take upwards of a year, I'm happy to say CCC seems to have sped way the hell up with their permitting. From application to pickup it looks to be about 3 months now. I had to delay the class a bit due to a scheduling conflict, but other than that picked the earliest times I could to complete each scheduled step. I'm happy to answer any questions or provide assistance with the process if anyone is interested.

Total cost including the 16 hour class: $611.25

2/2/25

Filed CCW application with Contra Costa County via Permitium ($80)

Waiting to hear back about when to do Livescan (called Sheriff and was instructed to do it immediately)
2/12/25

Completed Livescan ($123)

Called sheriff to confirm that I am now to wait until receiving email for next steps
2/18/25

References contacted
3/10/25

Received request for interview
3/12/25

Interview for CCW
3/17/25

Application approved contingent on training
4/6/25

CCW class completed ($328.25)

Submitted proof of training on CCC Permitium site
4/7/25

CCC sheriff requests appointment for range test
4/26/25

Sheriff Range Test - passed
4/29/25

Final payment requested - paid ($80)
5/2/25

Pick up permit

rows.title: Sac County CCW process and timeline…
rows.body:
Hello All.

This and the /CACCW sub have been so helpful to me. In an attempt to contribute to the community, I just wanted to disclose my CCW experiences and timeline so that it might help others in their process.

Initial Application to Sac County Sherriff(SCS) via Permitium portal: 22 Nov 2024

Supplementary Documentation requested by SCS: 5th December 2024

Interview Date set for: April 29
th 2025

On the 4th of Jan 2025 I noticed an appointment appeared for 29th January. Selected this to replace my original 29th April date.

SCS contacts my spouse, and two references on the 8th of January 2025. On the same day SCS requests updated proof of address (original ones had since passed the 60 day window required for validity).

SCS confirms interview time and date on the 9th January.

SCS interview was with a retired deputy. Cordial, calm and informal. Total time was 21 mins. Interviewer says I can take Livescan once interview is over. Since the Livescan is in the same building, I just happened to see an opening 40 mins after the interview. Submitted the Livescan that same day.

On the 30th Jan 2025, SCS confirms receipt of Livescan and says I can now complete the 16hr CCW Training requirement. Found and registered for a class held on 13th/14th Feb 2025. Submitted certificates to SCS on the same day.

SCS contacts me on 17th of Feb 2025 confirming receipt of document and requests final fee payment. Paid it that day and received final email to pick up permit any Tue / Thurs.

18th Feb 2025, pick up and sign for permit.

Costs:

Application fee: $58 Livescan : $145 16hr CCW Course: $225 Final SCS fee: $191

TOTAL: $619

Start Date: 22 Nov 2024 End Date: 18 Feb 2025

Happy to answer any questions in this thread of via direct message.

rows.title: FINALLY!!! CCW Timeline Kern
rows.body:
Finally picked up my permit today and I couldn't be more excited about it! From prohibited person for 12 years, to now CCW holder, it feels great. Huge milestone for me in my life. So many "friends" and family doubted me, and I'm happy AF to prove them all wrong.

I know everyone here loves timelines so here is mine for Kern County:

9/30/24 - Submitted my application online through Permitium for Kern Sheriff. ($169)
10/8/24 - Got the call to come in for brief interview.
10/14/24 - Interview appointment. All they did was just review my application and ask a few follow up questions.
10/23/24 - Update in Permitium, conditional approval to sign up for CCW Training. (16 hours)
10/23/24 - Enrolled in CCW training. ($275) Did like 10 hours of training online, finished last 6 hours at range.
11/2/24 - CCW range session and qualifications.
11/4/24 - Submitted qualification card and did a final Live Scan at Sheriff office.
2/4/25 - Live Scan finally completed. (This was the longest process just over 3 months)
2/6/25 - Got call from Sheriff I've been officially approved.
2/10/25 - Appointment to pick up my permit.

All in all, it took just a little over 4 months from start to finish. Total out of pocket cost was $444 plus the cost of ammo for training and qualification. I owe a big thanks to lot of people on Reddit and a few of my coworkers for assisting me throughout the process.
